Package org.rodinp.core

Interface IRodinFile

All Superinterfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IElementManipulation, IOpenable, IParent, IRodinElement, ISnapshotable
All Known Implementing Classes:
RodinFile
public interface IRodinFile extends IOpenable, IParent, IElementManipulation, ISnapshotable
Represents an entire Rodin file. File elements need to be opened before they can be navigated or manipulated.

For each Rodin file, the database provides two versions:

  • a stable snapshot that corresponds to the contents of the Rodin file on disk, and which is read-only.
  • a buffered copy of the Rodin file in memory which is read-write.
As a consequence, there are two kinds of handles for these elements, stable snapshot handles and mutable handles.

Each file has a root element, which type is determined from the extension of the Rodin file. The associations between file extensions and root element types are declared with plugin.xml extensions.

Since:
1.0

  • Field Details

    • ELEMENT_TYPE

      static final IElementType<IRodinFile> ELEMENT_TYPE
      The element type of all Rodin Files.

  • Method Details

    • create

      void create(boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Creates this file in the database. As a side effect, all ancestors of this element are open if they were not already.

      It is possible that this file already exists. The value of the force parameter effects the resolution of such a conflict:

      • true - in this case the file is created anew with empty contents
      • false - in this case a RodinDBException is thrown

      Parameters:
      force - specify how to handle conflict is this element already exists
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the element could not be created. Reasons include:
      • The parent of this element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • This file element exists and force is false (NAME_COLLISION)
      • A CoreException occurred while creating an underlying resource

    • findElements

      @Deprecated(since="1.10") IRodinElement[] findElements(IRodinElement element)
      Deprecated.
      This method has never been implemented and always returns an empty array.
      Finds the elements in this file that correspond to the given element. An element A corresponds to an element B if:
      • A has the same element type and name as B.
      • The parent of A corresponds to the parent of B recursively up to their respective files.
      • A exists.
      Returns null if no such Rodin elements can be found or if the given element is not included in a file.
      Parameters:
      element - the given element
      Returns:
      the found elements in this file that correspond to the given element

    • getBareName

      String getBareName()
      Returns the bare name of this Rodin file. The bare name of a Rodin file is its element name with the possible file extension removed.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the bare name of this Rodin file
      See Also:
      • IResource.getFileExtension()

    • getResource

      org.eclipse.core.resources.IFile getResource()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the innermost resource enclosing this element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getResource in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the innermost resource enclosing this element

    • revert

      void revert() throws RodinDBException
      Reverts this file. Reverting a file has the effect of forgetting any unsaved change and closing the file.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error occurs closing this element

    • getRoot

      IInternalElement getRoot()
      Returns the root internal element of this file.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the root element of this Rodin file

    • getRootElementType

      IInternalElementType<?> getRootElementType()
      Returns the element type of the root element of this file.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Returns:
      the type of the root element of this Rodin file

    • getSnapshot

      IRodinFile getSnapshot()
      Description copied from interface: ISnapshotable
      Returns a handle to this element in the snapshot of its Rodin file.

      This is a handle-only method. The element may or may not be present.

      Specified by:
      getSnapshot in interface ISnapshotable
      Returns:
      this element in the snapshot of its Rodin file

    • getMutableCopy

      IRodinFile getMutableCopy()
      Description copied from interface: ISnapshotable
      Returns a handle to this element in the mutable copy of its Rodin file.

      This is a handle-only method. The element may or may not be present.

      Specified by:
      getMutableCopy in interface ISnapshotable
      Returns:
      this element in the mutable copy of its Rodin file
